entries.preparationInstructions: Remove all jewelry and metallic objects from the chest area. Wear a hospital gown.
entries.positioningInstructions: PA (posteroanterior) and lateral views. Patient standing upright, deep inspiration.

entries.preparationInstructions: Remove all jewelry and metallic objects from the chest area. Wear a hospital gown.
entries.positioningInstructions: PA (posteroanterior) view. Patient standing upright, deep inspiration.

entries.preparationInstructions: Remove belt, jewelry, and metallic objects. No bowel preparation typically required for standard KUB.
entries.positioningInstructions: Supine AP view. Include kidneys, ureters, and bladder.

entries.preparationInstructions: Remove rings, bracelets, and all jewelry from the hand and wrist.
entries.positioningInstructions: PA, oblique, and lateral views. Hand flat on cassette, fingers spread.

entries.preparationInstructions: Remove all metallic objects from head and neck (earrings, hair clips, dentures). No fasting required.
entries.positioningInstructions: Supine, head in head holder. Scan from skull base to vertex. Axial slices parallel to orbitomeatal line.

entries.preparationInstructions: Check renal function (eGFR). NPO 4 hours prior. Assess for contrast allergy. Discontinue metformin if applicable.
entries.positioningInstructions: Supine, head in head holder. Pre-contrast and post-contrast axial images from skull base to vertex.
entries.facilityRequirements: CT suite with contrast injection capability

entries.studyName: CT Abdomen and Pelvis with Contrast
entries.contrastType: Iodinated IV contrast and/or oral contrast
entries.preparationInstructions: NPO 4-6 hours prior. Oral contrast 1-2 hours before scan if ordered. Check renal function. Assess for contrast allergy. Hydrate well.
entries.positioningInstructions: Supine, arms above head. Scan from diaphragm to pubic symphysis.
entries.facilityRequirements: CT suite with contrast injection capability

entries.preparationInstructions: Remove all metallic objects from chest. Wear a hospital gown. Breath-hold instructions will be given.
entries.positioningInstructions: Supine, arms above head. Scan from lung apices to adrenal glands. Full inspiration breath-hold.

entries.preparationInstructions: Complete MRI safety screening questionnaire. Remove all metallic objects, jewelry, and electronic devices. No fasting required.
entries.positioningInstructions: Supine, head in head coil. Axial, sagittal, and coronal sequences. T1, T2, FLAIR, DWI minimum.

entries.studyName: MRI Brain with and without Contrast
entries.preparationInstructions: Complete MRI safety screening questionnaire. Check renal function (risk of NSF with gadolinium). Remove all metallic objects. IV access required.
entries.positioningInstructions: Supine, head in head coil. Pre- and post-contrast sequences. T1, T2, FLAIR, DWI, post-contrast T1 with fat saturation.
contraindications: Severe renal impairment (risk of NSF with gadolinium)
entries.facilityRequirements: MRI suite with appropriate shielding, contrast injection capability
entries.equipmentRequirements: MRI scanner (minimum 1.5T, preferably 3T), head coil, MRI-compatible power injector

entries.preparationInstructions: Complete MRI safety screening questionnaire. Remove all metallic objects. No fasting required.
entries.positioningInstructions: Supine, spine coil. Sagittal and axial images through lumbar spine. T1, T2, STIR sequences.

entries.preparationInstructions: Complete MRI safety screening questionnaire. Remove all metallic objects from the leg. No fasting required.
entries.positioningInstructions: Supine, knee in dedicated knee coil. Sagittal, coronal, and axial images. PD, T2 FS, T1 sequences.

entries.preparationInstructions: NPO (nothing by mouth) for 6-8 hours prior to examination. Water permitted. Full bladder preferred.
entries.positioningInstructions: Supine and decubitus positions. Evaluate liver, gallbladder, bile ducts, pancreas, spleen, kidneys, and aorta.
entries.equipmentRequirements: Ultrasound machine with curvilinear transducer (2-5 MHz)

entries.preparationInstructions: Full bladder required for transabdominal approach. Drink 32 oz of water 1 hour prior. Do not void before exam.
entries.positioningInstructions: Supine for transabdominal. Evaluate uterus, ovaries, adnexa, and bladder.
entries.equipmentRequirements: Ultrasound machine with curvilinear (2-5 MHz) and endocavitary transducer (5-9 MHz)

entries.preparationInstructions: No special preparation required. Remove necklaces and scarves.
entries.positioningInstructions: Supine with neck slightly extended (pillow under shoulders). Evaluate both thyroid lobes, isthmus, and cervical lymph nodes.
entries.equipmentRequirements: Ultrasound machine with high-frequency linear transducer (7-15 MHz)

entries.preparationInstructions: No special preparation required. Remove necklaces.
entries.positioningInstructions: Supine, head turned away from the side being examined. Evaluate bilateral common carotid, internal carotid, external carotid, and vertebral arteries.
entries.equipmentRequirements: Ultrasound machine with linear transducer (5-12 MHz) and Doppler capability

entries.preparationInstructions: Do not apply deodorant, powder, or lotion under arms or on breasts on the day of the exam. Schedule preferably 1 week after menstruation.
entries.positioningInstructions: Standing or seated. CC (craniocaudal) and MLO (mediolateral oblique) views of each breast.
entries.equipmentRequirements: Digital mammography unit (preferably with tomosynthesis capability)

entries.preparationInstructions: Do not apply deodorant, powder, or lotion under arms or on breasts on the day of the exam. Bring prior mammograms for comparison.
entries.positioningInstructions: Standing or seated. CC and MLO views plus additional views as needed (spot compression, magnification, rolled views).
entries.equipmentRequirements: Digital mammography unit with spot compression and magnification capability
